item.title: Welcome Prof. Yuhan Liu (HIT) for a research visit
item.description:
We warmly welcome Prof. Yuhan Liu, Associate Professor from Harbin Institute of Technology (HIT), for a research visit to SCLABLE in July 2026.

Armin Lederer, Lei Zheng, Zhihua Wu, and Tharusha Fonseka look forward to meeting with Prof. Liu to discuss learning-based control, robotics, and safe autonomy.

Learn more about Prof. Liu at https://homepage.hit.edu.cn/liuyuhan?lang=en.

item.title: Three papers accepted at ICML 2026
item.description: We got three papers accepted at the International Conference on Machine Learning. You can already check out the preprints here, here and here. Looking forward to discussing the papers with you in Seoul!

item.title: Two papers accepted at IEEE TAC
item.description: Two papers recently got accepted at the IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control. Check out the papers here and here.

item.title: Papers accepted at ECC and ACC 2026
item.description: We got papers accepted at the European Control Conference and the American Control Conference. Check the ECC preprint out here.

item.title: Koopman workshop at CDC was a success
item.description: The workshop on Koopman operator theory at the IEEE Conference on Decision and Control co-organized with Igor Mezic, Karl Worthmann, Petar Bevanda and Sandra Hirche was a great success. Presentation slides are available on the conference website.

item.title: Two papers accepted at CDC 2025
item.description: Two papers got accepted at the IEEE Conference on Decision and Control: “Distributed Risk-Sensitive Safety Filters for Uncertain Discrete-Time Systems” and “Learning High-Order CBFs using Gaussian Processes for Systems in Brunovsky Canonical Form”.

item.title: L-CSS paper on distributed risk-sensitive safety filters
item.description: Our paper “Distributed Risk-Sensitive Safety Filters for Uncertain Discrete-Time Systems” got accepted to the IEEE Control Systems Letters. Check out the paper here.

item.title: TAC paper on risk-sensitive safety filters for RL
item.description: Our paper “Risk-Sensitive Safety Filters for Reinforcement Learning with Probabilistic Guarantees” has been accepted to the IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control. Check out the paper here.

item.title: Armin Lederer joins NUS ECE as Assistant Professor
item.description: Armin Lederer has started as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering at the National University of Singapore, where he leads the Safe and Control Learning Lab (SCLABLE).
